sources.notes: Construct an attack sequence so exactly m elves survive.
Independent writeup explains sorting by attack, the 2m <= n condition, and the m=0 special case.

annotation.summary: Construct attacks among distinct-strength elves so that exactly m remain alive, or report impossible.
annotation.constraints: n up to 2e5, so the construction must be sorting plus linear output.
annotation.core_idea: Sort elves by strength. If 2m > n, every survivor would need a weaker victim but there are not enough victims. For m > 0, let the strongest m kill the next m, and let remaining weaker elves attack a stronger neighbor to die by recoil. For m = 0, the strongest cannot be killed by a stronger elf, so all other elves must accumulate enough damage on it; if their total strength is insufficient, impossible.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) for sorting plus O(n) output.
tricks: special all-dead case by accumulated damage

solution_variants.name: sorted-pair-construction
solution_variants.summary: Sort strengths, pair the strongest m survivors with the next m victims, and handle m=0 by surrounding the maximum.

tags.evidence: The task asks for an explicit attack sequence and the solution directly constructs one.
The construction greedily makes the strongest m survive and assigns them the next m victims.
All pairings are defined by sorted strength order.
The m=0 case needs a separate accumulated-damage construction for the strongest elf.
tags.description: A solution whose main construction works broadly but one boundary case requires a distinct argument or construction.
tags.created_reason: Boundary-case construction is a recurring pattern worth distinguishing from generic implementation.
